  name: string;
  stage: string;
  sub_type?: string;
  content: string;
  variables?: string[];
}
```

---

### 4. **Activity Log Popup for Messages**

**State Definition (Lines 263-281):**
```typescript
const [activityLogPrompt, setActivityLogPrompt] = useState<{
  oppId: string;
  oppName: string;
  type: ActivityType;  // 'call' | 'message' | 'meeting' | 'proposal' | 'note' | 'bump'
  title: string;
  showMeetingPicker?: boolean;
  ownerId?: string;
} | null>(null);

const [activityLogForm, setActivityLogForm] = useState<{
  title: string;
  description: string;
  outcome: string;
  next_step: string;
  next_step_date?: string;
  meeting_date?: string;
  meeting_time?: string;
}>({ title: '', description: '', outcome: '', next_step: '' });
```

**Type Definition in Types (Line 18):**
```typescript
export type ActivityType = 'call' | 'message' | 'meeting' | 'proposal' | 'note' | 'bump';
```

**When Message Type is Set (Line 1409):**
```typescript
// From handleActionExecute when action.actionType === 'bump'
openActivityLogPrompt(oppId, opp.name, 'message', bumpTitle);
```

**Dialog Render (Lines 4129-4389):**
- Type badge with icon (Lines 4161-4169)
- Title input (Lines 4171-4180)
- Conditional booking link section (Lines 4183-4212) - **Only shown for messages with showMeetingPicker**
- Meeting date/time pickers (Lines 4214-4308) - **Only shown if showMeetingPicker is true**
- Description textarea (Lines 4311-4321)
- Outcome input (Lines 4324-4332)
- Next step input (Lines 4334-4341)
- Next step date picker (Lines 4345-4372)

---

### 5. **How handleActionExecute Opens Activity Form**

**Function Definition (Lines 1402-1446):**

```typescript
const handleActionExecute = async (
  oppId: string, 
  action: ReturnType<typeof getNextAction>, 
  opp: SalesPipelineOpportunity
) => {
  setExecutingAction(oppId);
  try {
    if (action.actionType === 'bump') {
      await handleRecordBump(oppId);
      // Show activity log popup after bump
      const bumpTitle = action.label === 'Send First DM' ? 'First DM sent' : `${action.label} sent`;
      openActivityLogPrompt(oppId, opp.name, 'message', bumpTitle);  // Line 1409
    } else if (action.actionType === 'stage_change' && action.targetStage) {
      await handleStageChange(oppId, action.targetStage, opp.stage);
      // Maps stages to activity types
      const stageActivityMap: Record<string, { type: ActivityType; title: string; showMeetingPicker?: boolean }> = {
        'warm': { type: 'message', title: 'KOL replied' },  // Line 1416
        'booked': { type: 'meeting', title: 'Meeting booked', showMeetingPicker: true },
        // ... more stages
      };
      const mapping = stageActivityMap[action.targetStage];
      if (mapping) {
        openActivityLogPrompt(oppId, opp.name, mapping.type, mapping.title, mapping.showMeetingPicker, opp.owner_id || undefined);
      }
    } else {
      // open_detail actions
      if (action.label === 'Book Next Meeting!' || action.label === 'Schedule Meeting') {
        openActivityLogPrompt(oppId, opp.name, 'meeting', 'Meeting scheduled', true, opp.owner_id || undefined);
      }
      // ...
    }
  } finally {
    setExecutingAction(null);
  }
};
```

**Open Function (Lines 1397-1400):**
```typescript
const openActivityLogPrompt = (
  oppId: string, 
  oppName: string, 
  type: ActivityType, 
  title: string, 
  showMeetingPicker?: boolean, 
  ownerId?: string
) => {
  setActivityLogPrompt({ oppId, oppName, type, title, showMeetingPicker, ownerId });
  setActivityLogForm({ 
    title, 
    description: '', 
    outcome: '', 
    next_step: '', 
    meeting_date: undefined, 
    meeting_time: undefined, 
    next_step_date: undefined 
  });
};
```

**Confirm Function (Lines 1448-1483):**
- Creates activity record via `SalesPipelineService.createActivity()`
- Updates `last_contacted_at` timestamp
- If meeting picker was shown, updates `next_meeting_at` with selected date/time

---

### Key Files

1. **Type Definitions:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/salesPipelineService.ts` (lines 18, 100-193)
2. **Service Methods:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/salesPipelineService.ts` (lines 682-732)
3. **UI Implementation:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx` (lines 1-5493)
4. **Imports:** Lines 1-80, 51-75 specifically for types and services

2. **Step 1 - Migration**: Created `client_action_items` table with columns: id (uuid PK), client_id (FK), text, court ('yours'|'ours'), phase ('kickoff'|'discovery'|'tracker'), is_done, is_hidden, display_order, created_at, updated_at. RLS: authenticated full CRUD, anon SELECT.

3. **Step 2-4 - Admin page changes** (`app/clients/page.tsx`):
   - Added `ActionItem` type
   - Added state variables: clientActionItems, actionItemForm, editingActionItemId, isActionItemFormOpen, deletingActionItemId, contextModalTab, actionBoardPhase
   - Added functions: refreshActionItems(), seedActionItems(), handleActionItemSubmit(), toggleActionItemDone(), toggleActionItemHidden(), deleteActionItem()
   - Added DEFAULT_ACTION_ITEMS array with 14 default items
   - Converted Context modal to tabbed layout (Context tab + Action Board tab)
   - Action Board tab has phase sub-tabs and Your Court/Our Court sections
   - Added Eye, EyeOff imports

4. **Step 5 - Portal changes** (`app/public/portal/[id]/page.tsx`):
   - Added actionItems and showCompleted state
   - Added fetchActionItems() function
   - Replaced hardcoded actionBoardItems with DB-backed items + fallback
   - Added "Show completed" toggle

5. **User shared onboarding guide**: User showed an HTML document with a 7-day onboarding timeline. I updated the DEFAULT_ACTION_ITEMS to match this guide (22 items across 3 phases) and updated the portal fallback items to match.

6. **Portal design improvement**: User asked to make Your Court/Our Court look better (portal only). I redesigned with:
   - Gradient top accent bars
   - Progress bars with counters
   - Subtitle text ("Awaiting your action" / "We're on it")
   - Circular checkboxes
   - Better hover states
   - "All caught up!" empty state
   - Eye/EyeOff icons on toggle
   - Added EyeOff import to portal

7. **Attachment support for action items**: User wanted links/files on action items.
   - Migration: Added attachment_url and attachment_label columns to client_action_items
   - Updated ActionItem type, form state, CRUD handlers

   - Portal: Updated fetch query, passed attachment data through, rendered clickable links in both courts

8. **POC in actions table**: User asked to add POC platform and handle to the sales pipeline actions table.
   - Added POC column header between Name and Stage
   - Added POC cell rendering with Badge + handle text
   - Updated colSpan for empty state from 8 to 9

9. **Clean POC handles**: User wanted URLs stripped from POC handles and fix slide-over overflow.
   - Added `cleanPocHandle()` helper that strips common URL prefixes (x.com, twitter.com, instagram.com, linkedin.com, t.me, discord.gg)
   - Applied to all POC display locations (actions table, outreach table, slide-over)
   - Fixed slide-over: added max-w-[calc(100vw-2rem)], overflow-hidden, min-w-0 on detail grid cells, truncate on handles

10. **Activity timeline overflow**: User showed screenshot of side menu overflowing due to long URLs.
    - Added overflow-hidden, flex-wrap on activity item container
    - Added `overflow-wrap: anywhere` and `break-words` to description, outcome, next_step
    - Fixed notes section with same treatment
    - Fixed quick actions row with flex-wrap

11. **Stage dropdown in New Opportunity**: Added stage Select dropdown (using ALL_V2_STAGES) to the create dialog, only shown for new (not edit), defaulting to 'cold_dm'.

12. **Clickable links in timeline**: Added `linkifyText()` helper that converts URLs to clickable `<a>` tags. Applied to title, description, outcome, and next_step fields in activity timeline.

13. **Hide call option**: Removed `<SelectItem value="call">Call</SelectItem>` from the activity type dropdown in the slide-over timeline form.

14. **File upload for activity timeline**:

    - Added Paperclip icon import
    - Updated handleAddActivity to upload file to storage before creating activity
    - Added file picker UI (hidden input + button + filename display)
    - Added attachment display in timeline items

15. **Current/pending task**: User asked about orbit timing (answered: 90 days based on updated_at). Then asked to show DM templates in the activity log popup for message type, with default template matching the opportunity's current stage.

The agent explored the template system and found:

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user requested a series of incremental features for a KOL Campaign Manager built with Next.js and Supabase:
   - **Database-backed Action Board**: Replace hardcoded Your Court/Our Court items with a DB-driven system. Admins can CRUD items; portal is read-only with show/hide completed toggle.
   - **Better default templates**: Update seed items based on a 7-day onboarding guide document the user shared.
   - **Portal design improvement**: Improve the visual design of the action board cards in the client portal (progress bars, counters, better styling).
   - **Attachment support on action items**: Allow admins to attach a link (URL + label) to each action item, visible in portal.
   - **POC in sales pipeline actions table**: Add POC platform and handle columns to the actions table.
   - **Clean POC handles**: Strip URL prefixes from POC handles in display, fix slide-over overflow issues.
   - **Fix activity timeline overflow**: Long URLs in activity timeline were stretching the slide-over panel.
   - **Stage dropdown in New Opportunity dialog**: Add stage selection when creating new opportunities.
   - **Clickable links in activity timeline**: Make URLs in activity text clickable.
   - **Hide call option**: Remove "Call" from activity type dropdown.
   - **File upload for activity timeline**: Allow file attachments when logging activities.
   - **DM templates in message activity log popup**: Show templates when logging message activities, defaulting to the opportunity's current stage (CURRENT/PENDING TASK).

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Next.js App Router with `'use client'` components
   - Supabase (PostgreSQL, RLS policies, Storage buckets, MCP migration tool)
   - React state management with `useState`, `useRef`, `useMemo`, `useCallback`
   - Shadcn UI components (Dialog, Tabs, Select, Badge, Card, etc.)
   - Lucide React icons
   - Pattern: `Record<clientId, T[]>` for grouped data
   - Pattern: seed defaults on first access, fallback for un-seeded clients
   - Pattern: inline CRUD with two-step delete confirmation
   - Supabase Storage for file uploads with public buckets
   - URL linkification via regex splitting

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`app/clients/page.tsx`** (Admin clients page — ~3000 lines)
     - Core file for admin-side action board management
     - Added `ActionItem` type (lines 84-97):
       ```typescript
       type ActionItem = {
         id: string;
         client_id: string;
         text: string;
         court: 'yours' | 'ours';
         phase: 'kickoff' | 'discovery' | 'tracker';
         is_done: boolean;
         is_hidden: boolean;
         display_order: number;
         attachment_url: string | null;
         attachment_label: string | null;
         created_at: string;
         updated_at: string;
       };
       ```
     - Added state variables after weekly updates state:
       ```typescript
       const [clientActionItems, setClientActionItems] = useState<Record<string, ActionItem[]>>({});
       const [actionItemForm, setActionItemForm] = useState<{ text: string; court: 'yours' | 'ours'; attachment_url: string; attachment_label: string }>({ text: '', court: 'yours', attachment_url: '', attachment_label: '' });
       const [editingActionItemId, setEditingActionItemId] = useState<string | null>(null);
       const [isActionItemFormOpen, setIsActionItemFormOpen] = useState(false);
       const [deletingActionItemId, setDeletingActionItemId] = useState<string | null>(null);
       const [contextModalTab, setContextModalTab] = useState<string>('context');
       const [actionBoardPhase, setActionBoardPhase] = useState<string>('kickoff');
       ```
     - Added functions: `refreshActionItems()`, `seedActionItems()`, `handleActionItemSubmit()` (with attachment support), `toggleActionItemDone()`, `toggleActionItemHidden()`, `deleteActionItem()`
     - `DEFAULT_ACTION_ITEMS` array with 22 items based on the 7-day onboarding guide across kickoff (6), discovery (8), tracker (8)
     - Converted Context modal to use `<Tabs>` with "Context" and "Action Board" tabs
     - Action Board tab has nested phase sub-tabs (kickoff/discovery/tracker), items grouped by court, inline edit/add form with attachment fields
     - Added `Eye, EyeOff` to lucide imports
     - Added `LinkIcon` usage for attachment display on items

   - **`app/public/portal/[id]/page.tsx`** (Client portal — ~2300 lines)
     - Added state:
       ```typescript
       const [actionItems, setActionItems] = useState<{ id: string; text: string; court: string; phase: string; is_done: boolean; display_order: number; attachment_url: string | null; attachment_label: string | null }[]>([]);
       const [showCompleted, setShowCompleted] = useState(false);
       ```
     - Added `fetchActionItems()` fetching from DB with `is_hidden = false` filter, including attachment fields
     - Replaced hardcoded `actionBoardItems` with `fallbackActionBoardItems` (updated to match 7-day guide) + DB-backed `actionBoardItems` with attachment passthrough
     - Redesigned action board cards with: gradient accent bars, progress bars, counter badges, subtitles, circular checkboxes, better hover states, "All caught up!" empty state
     - Added show/hide completed toggle with Eye/EyeOff icons
     - Both Your Court and Our Court items render attachment links when present (orange-themed for yours, teal for ours)
     - Added `EyeOff` to lucide imports

   - **`app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx`** (Sales pipeline — ~5400 lines)
     - Added POC column to actions table between Name and Stage with `cleanPocHandle()` display
     - `cleanPocHandle()` helper (after `getUserName`):
       ```typescript
       const cleanPocHandle = (handle: string): string => {
         return handle
           .replace(/^https?:\/\/(www\.)?(x\.com|twitter\.com|instagram\.com|linkedin\.com\/in|t\.me|discord\.gg|discord\.com\/users)\/?/i, '@')
           .replace(/^https?:\/\/(www\.)?[^/]+\/?/i, '')
           .replace(/\/+$/, '');
       };
       ```
     - Applied `cleanPocHandle` to all 3 POC display locations (actions table, outreach table, slide-over)
     - Fixed slide-over overflow: `max-w-[calc(100vw-2rem)]`, `overflow-hidden`, `min-w-0` on grid cells
     - Fixed activity timeline overflow: `overflow-wrap: anywhere`, `break-words`, `flex-wrap` on title/badge row
     - Fixed notes section, quick actions row with same overflow treatment
     - Added stage dropdown to New Opportunity dialog (only for create, not edit):
       ```jsx
       {!isEdit && (
         <div className="grid gap-2">
           <Label>Stage</Label>
           <Select value={form.stage || 'cold_dm'} onValueChange={v => setForm(f => ({ ...f, stage: v as SalesPipelineStage }))}>
             ...ALL_V2_STAGES options...
           </Select>
         </div>
       )}
       ```
     - Added `linkifyText()` helper:
       ```typescript
       const linkifyText = (text: string) => {
         const urlRegex = /(https?:\/\/[^\s]+)/g;
         const parts = text.split(urlRegex);
         if (parts.length === 1) return text;
         return parts.map((part, i) =>
           urlRegex.test(part) ? (
             <a key={i} href={part} target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" className="text-[#3e8692] hover:underline break-all" onClick={e => e.stopPropagation()}>{part}</a>
           ) : part
         );
       };
       ```
     - Applied `linkifyText` to activity timeline title, description, outcome, next_step
     - Removed "Call" option from activity type dropdown
     - Added file upload for activities:
       - State: `activityFile`, `activityFileRef`
       - Added `Paperclip` icon import
       - Updated `handleAddActivity` to upload file to `crm-attachments` bucket before creating activity
       - Added file picker UI with attach button, filename display, remove button
       - Added attachment display in timeline items with Paperclip icon link
     - Updated colSpan from 8 to 9 for empty state in actions table

   - **`lib/salesPipelineService.ts`** (Service layer)
     - Updated `CRMActivity` interface to add `attachment_url: string | null` and `attachment_name: string | null`
     - Updated `CreateActivityData` interface to add `attachment_url?: string` and `attachment_name?: string`
     - Contains `SalesDmTemplate` interface (lines 100-111) with fields: id, name, stage, sub_type, content, variables, is_active, created_by, created_at, updated_at
     - Templates are fetched via `SalesPipelineService.getTemplates()` and stored in `templates` state

   - **Supabase Migrations** (via MCP tool):
     - `create_client_action_items`: Created table with all columns, index, RLS policies
     - `add_attachment_to_action_items`: Added attachment_url, attachment_label to client_action_items
     - `add_attachment_to_crm_activities`: Added attachment_url, attachment_name to crm_activities
     - `create_crm_attachments_bucket`: Created public storage bucket with RLS policies

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **TypeScript compilation errors on first check**: Running `npx tsc --noEmit` showed module resolution errors for `@/components/...` paths — these were pre-existing config issues, not from changes. Verified by running `npx next build` which passed successfully.
   - **Slide-over overflow** (user reported with screenshot): Long Google Docs URLs in activity timeline were pushing the panel wider than viewport. Fixed with `overflow-wrap: anywhere`, `break-words`, `overflow-hidden`, `max-w-[calc(100vw-2rem)]` on the panel, and `min-w-0` on grid cells.
   - **POC handles showing full URLs**: User explicitly said "make it so that it takes out the link in the table if the user added in the poc." Fixed by creating `cleanPocHandle()` that strips common social media URL prefixes.

5. Problem Solving:
   - All builds verified passing after each change (`npx next build`)
   - Supabase security advisors checked — no new issues from migrations
   - RLS pattern copied from existing `client_meeting_notes` table
   - Storage bucket pattern copied from existing `client-logos` upload code
   - Fallback pattern for un-seeded clients ensures backward compatibility
   - Orbit timing question answered: 90 days based on `updated_at` field (line 1229-1237)

6. All User Messages:
   - "Implement the following plan: [detailed 5-step plan for Database-Backed Action Board]"
   - "Look at this. Does this help you set a better template/preset for us to work with?" (shared onboarding guide HTML)
   - "Can you make it so that the your court and our court design looks better?" (portal only)
   - "With each actions, can you make it so that we can add a link or file for them to view that they are finished in the admin page? This can show in the portal too."
   - "Can you add the poc platform and the handle/id in the actions table in the sales-pipeline page?"
   - "Can you make it so that it takes out the link in the table if the user added in the poc? Like do not put https://x.com, etc. Also the side menu overflows. Check this and make sure this doesnt happen"
   - "I think the activity time line makes it so it stretches the side menu. make it so it goes to next line when it has no space. I've attached a screenshot." (with screenshot)
   - "In the new opportunity popup, add the stage dropdown as well"
   - "Can you make it so that links in the activity time line is clickable?"
   - "Hide call option in the activity timeline"
   - "Can you add a file upload for the activity timeline?"
   - "How many days do orbits need to be in for them to require action to reach out again"
   - "For message activities (when the log activity popup shows up for message), can you make it so the templates show up and user can pick if they want to. Make the default message from the template so that it is from the same stage initially."

7. Pending Tasks:
   - **DM templates in message activity log popup**: Show templates when the activity log popup appears for message type. Default to templates matching the opportunity's current stage. User should be able to pick a template to pre-fill the message.

8. Current Work:
   The user's most recent request is: "For message activities (when the log activity popup shows up for message), can you make it so the templates show up and user can pick if they want to. Make the default message from the template so that it is from the same stage initially."

   An Explore agent was dispatched to understand the template system. Key findings:
   - Templates are in `templates` state (type `SalesDmTemplate[]`), fetched via `SalesPipelineService.getTemplates()`
   - Each template has `stage`, `sub_type`, `content`, `name` fields
   - The activity log popup is rendered by `renderActivityLogPrompt()` at line 4129 in `app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx`
   - The popup is opened via `openActivityLogPrompt(oppId, oppName, type, title, showMeetingPicker, ownerId)` at line 1397
   - For messages, it's triggered from `handleActionExecute` when `action.actionType === 'bump'` (line 1409)
   - The popup state includes `activityLogPrompt` (with oppId, type, etc.) and `activityLogForm` (with title, description, outcome, next_step)
   - The opportunity object is available via `activityLogPrompt.oppId` to look up the current stage
   
   No code changes have been made yet for this task. The exploration was completed but implementation has not started.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Implement the DM template selector in the activity log popup for message type activities. Based on the user's request: "For message activities (when the log activity popup shows up for message), can you make it so the templates show up and user can pick if they want to. Make the default message from the template so that it is from the same stage initially."

   Implementation plan:
   - In `renderActivityLogPrompt()`, when `activityLogPrompt?.type === 'message'`, show a template selector dropdown
   - Filter `templates` by the opportunity's current stage (look up opp from `opportunities` using `activityLogPrompt.oppId`)
   - When a template is selected, populate the description field with the template content
   - Auto-select the first matching template as default on popup open (in `openActivityLogPrompt` or via useEffect)
   - Key locations: `openActivityLogPrompt` (line 1397), `renderActivityLogPrompt` (line 4129), `activityLogPrompt` state (line 263), `templates` state (line 255)

     - Added `LinkIcon` usage for attachment display on items

   - **`app/public/portal/[id]/page.tsx`** (Client portal — ~2300 lines)
     - Added state:

       const [showCompleted, setShowCompleted] = useState(false);
       ```
     - Added `fetchActionItems()` fetching from DB with `is_hidden = false` filter, including attachment fields
     - Replaced hardcoded `actionBoardItems` with `fallbackActionBoardItems` (updated to match 7-day guide) + DB-backed `actionBoardItems` with attachment passthrough
     - Redesigned action board cards with: gradient accent bars, progress bars, counter badges, subtitles, circular checkboxes, better hover states, "All caught up!" empty state
     - Added show/hide completed toggle with Eye/EyeOff icons
     - Both Your Court and Our Court items render attachment links when present (orange-themed for yours, teal for ours)
     - Added `EyeOff` to lucide imports

   - **`app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx`** (Sales pipeline — ~5400 lines)
     - Added POC column to actions table between Name and Stage with `cleanPocHandle()` display
     - `cleanPocHandle()` helper (after `getUserName`):
       ```typescript
       const cleanPocHandle = (handle: string): string => {
         return handle
           .replace(/^https?:\/\/(www\.)?(x\.com|twitter\.com|instagram\.com|linkedin\.com\/in|t\.me|discord\.gg|discord\.com\/users)\/?/i, '@')
           .replace(/^https?:\/\/(www\.)?[^/]+\/?/i, '')
           .replace(/\/+$/, '');
       };
       ```
     - Applied `cleanPocHandle` to all 3 POC display locations (actions table, outreach table, slide-over)
     - Fixed slide-over overflow: `max-w-[calc(100vw-2rem)]`, `overflow-hidden`, `min-w-0` on grid cells
     - Fixed activity timeline overflow: `overflow-wrap: anywhere`, `break-words`, `flex-wrap` on title/badge row
     - Fixed notes section, quick actions row with same overflow treatment
     - Added stage dropdown to New Opportunity dialog (only for create, not edit):
       ```jsx
       {!isEdit && (
         <div className="grid gap-2">
           <Label>Stage</Label>
           <Select value={form.stage || 'cold_dm'} onValueChange={v => setForm(f => ({ ...f, stage: v as SalesPipelineStage }))}>
             ...ALL_V2_STAGES options...
           </Select>
         </div>
       )}
       ```
     - Added `linkifyText()` helper:
       ```typescript
       const linkifyText = (text: string) => {
         const urlRegex = /(https?:\/\/[^\s]+)/g;
         const parts = text.split(urlRegex);
         if (parts.length === 1) return text;
         return parts.map((part, i) =>
           urlRegex.test(part) ? (
             <a key={i} href={part} target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" className="text-[#3e8692] hover:underline break-all" onClick={e => e.stopPropagation()}>{part}</a>
           ) : part
         );
       };
       ```
     - Applied `linkifyText` to activity timeline title, description, outcome, next_step
     - Removed "Call" option from activity type dropdown
     - Added file upload for activities:
       - State: `activityFile`, `activityFileRef`
       - Added `Paperclip` icon import
       - Updated `handleAddActivity` to upload file to `crm-attachments` bucket before creating activity
       - Added file picker UI with attach button, filename display, remove button
       - Added attachment display in timeline items with Paperclip icon link
     - Updated colSpan from 8 to 9 for empty state in actions table

   - **`lib/salesPipelineService.ts`** (Service layer)
     - Updated `CRMActivity` interface to add `attachment_url: string | null` and `attachment_name: string | null`
     - Updated `CreateActivityData` interface to add `attachment_url?: string` and `attachment_name?: string`
     - Contains `SalesDmTemplate` interface (lines 100-111) with fields: id, name, stage, sub_type, content, variables, is_active, created_by, created_at, updated_at

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **TypeScript compilation errors on first check**: Running `npx tsc --noEmit` showed module resolution errors for `@/components/...` paths — these were pre-existing config issues, not from changes. Verified by running `npx next build` which passed successfully.
   - **Slide-over overflow** (user reported with screenshot): Long Google Docs URLs in activity timeline were pushing the panel wider than viewport. Fixed with `overflow-wrap: anywhere`, `break-words`, `overflow-hidden`, `max-w-[calc(100vw-2rem)]` on the panel, and `min-w-0` on grid cells.
   - **POC handles showing full URLs**: User explicitly said "make it so that it takes out the link in the table if the user added in the poc." Fixed by creating `cleanPocHandle()` that strips common social media URL prefixes.

  1397→  const openActivityLogPrompt = (oppId: string, oppName: string, type: ActivityType, title: string, showMeetingPicker?: boolean, ownerId?: string) => {
  1398→    setActivityLogPrompt({ oppId, oppName, type, title, showMeetingPicker, ownerId });
  1399→    setActivityLogForm({ title, description: '', outcome: '', next_step: '', meeting_date: undefined, meeting_time: undefined, next_step_date: undefined });
  1400→  };

  1402→  const handleActionExecute = async (oppId: string, action: ReturnType<typeof getNextAction>, opp: SalesPipelineOpportunity) => {
  1403→    setExecutingAction(oppId);
  1404→    try {
  1405→      if (action.actionType === 'bump') {
  1406→        await handleRecordBump(oppId);
  1407→        // Show activity log popup after bump
  1408→        const bumpTitle = action.label === 'Send First DM' ? 'First DM sent' : `${action.label} sent`;
  1409→        openActivityLogPrompt(oppId, opp.name, 'message', bumpTitle);
  1410→      } else if (action.actionType === 'stage_change' && action.targetStage) {
  1411→        await handleStageChange(oppId, action.targetStage, opp.stage);
  1412→        // Skip activity log for stages that have their own prompts (orbit, closed_lost, tg_intro, discovery_done)
  1413→        const stagesWithOwnPrompts = ['orbit', 'v2_closed_lost', 'tg_intro', 'discovery_done'];
  1414→        if (!stagesWithOwnPrompts.includes(action.targetStage)) {
  1415→          const stageActivityMap: Record<string, { type: ActivityType; title: string; showMeetingPicker?: boolean }> = {
  1416→            'warm': { type: 'message', title: 'KOL replied' },
  1417→            'booked': { type: 'meeting', title: 'Meeting booked', showMeetingPicker: true },
  1418→            'proposal_call': { type: 'call', title: 'Proposal call set', showMeetingPicker: true },
  1419→            'v2_contract': { type: 'call', title: 'Moving to contract' },
  1420→            'v2_closed_won': { type: 'note', title: 'Deal closed won!' },
  1421→          };
  1422→          const mapping = stageActivityMap[action.targetStage];
  1423→          if (mapping) {
  1424→            openActivityLogPrompt(oppId, opp.name, mapping.type, mapping.title, mapping.showMeetingPicker, opp.owner_id || undefined);
  1425→          }
  1426→        }
  1427→      } else {
  1428→        // open_detail actions
  1429→        if (action.label === 'Book Next Meeting!' || action.label === 'Schedule Meeting') {
  1430→          openActivityLogPrompt(oppId, opp.name, 'meeting', 'Meeting scheduled', true, opp.owner_id || undefined);
  1431→        } else if (action.label === 'Log Meeting Outcome') {
  1432→          openActivityLogPrompt(oppId, opp.name, 'meeting', 'Meeting outcome', false);
  1433→        } else if (action.label === 'Send Proposal') {
  1434→          // Mark proposal as sent, then show activity log
  1435→          await SalesPipelineService.update(oppId, { proposal_sent_at: new Date().toISOString() } as any);
  1436→          openActivityLogPrompt(oppId, opp.name, 'proposal', 'Proposal sent');
  1437→          await fetchData();
  1438→        } else {
  1439→          const guidance = ACTION_GUIDANCE[action.label];

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-12 16:25:38Z)**_

Build passes. Here's what I implemented:

**DM Templates in Message Activity Log Popup:**

1. **Auto-fill on open** — When the activity log popup opens for a message type, it finds templates matching the opportunity's current stage and pre-fills the description with the first matching template's content.

2. **Template dropdown** — A "DM Template" select appears at the top of the popup (only for message type). It shows:
   - "No template" to clear the description
   - Templates matching the current stage grouped under a header
   - Other stage templates in a separate "Other Stages" section

3. **Better message UX** — The description label changes to "Message" for message type, placeholder becomes "DM content...", and the textarea gets more rows (4 instead of 2) to accommodate template content.

---

Perfect! I now have a comprehensive understanding of the changelog system. Let me create a final summary report.

## Changelog System Summary

I've found and analyzed the complete changelog system in this KOL Campaign Manager project. Here's what I discovered:

### 1. **Changelog-Related Files & Components**

#### Service Layer:
- **`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/changelogService.ts`** (260 lines)
  - Core service with TypeScript interfaces and database methods
  - Handles all CRUD operations for changelogs

#### Context & State Management:
- **`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/contexts/ChangelogContext.tsx`** (100 lines)
  - React Context provider managing global changelog state
  - Tracks published and unread changelogs
  - Prevents redundant API calls with fetch tracking

#### UI Components:
- **`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/changelog/ChangelogModal.tsx`** (200+ lines)
  - User-facing modal dialog for displaying unread changelogs
  - Supports navigation between multiple changelog entries
  - Simple markdown-like rendering (headers, bullet points, paragraphs)
  - "Mark as read" checkbox and dismiss functionality

#### Admin Page:
- **`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/admin/changelog/page.tsx`** (480+ lines)
  - Super admin-only management interface
  - Create, edit, delete, and publish changelogs
  - Draft/published status toggle
  - Integrates with Telegram notifications when publishing

### 2. **Database Storage**

The changelog system uses **two Supabase tables**:

#### `changelogs` table
Stores the changelog data with these columns (inferred from the service):
- `id` (UUID, primary key)
- `version` (string) - Version number like "1.2.0"
- `title` (string) - Changelog title
- `content` (string) - Changelog content (markdown-like format)
- `is_published` (boolean) - Publication status
- `published_at` (timestamp, nullable) - When the changelog was published
- `created_by` (string, nullable) - User ID of creator
- `created_at` (timestamp) - Creation timestamp
- `updated_at` (timestamp) - Last update timestamp

#### `changelog_views` table
Tracks which users have read which changelogs:
- `user_id` (UUID) - User who viewed the changelog
- `changelog_id` (UUID) - Changelog that was viewed
- `viewed_at` (timestamp) - When the user viewed it
- Composite unique constraint on `user_id` and `changelog_id`

**Note:** These tables don't have explicit migration files in `/supabase/migrations/`. They appear to be created directly in Supabase or via a manual SQL script not tracked in this migrations folder.

### 3. **Changelog Structure & Format**

**Content Format:** Simple markdown-like syntax
```
## Section Headers
### Subsection Headers
- Bullet point 1
- Bullet point 2
Regular paragraph text
```

**Changelog Entry Example:**
```typescript
{
  id: "uuid-string",
  version: "1.2.0",
  title: "New Features & Improvements",
  content: "## New Features\n- Feature 1\n- Feature 2\n\n## Bug Fixes\n- Fix description",
  is_published: true,
  published_at: "2026-03-13T10:00:00Z",
  created_by: "user-uuid",
  created_at: "2026-03-12T15:30:00Z",
  updated_at: "2026-03-13T10:00:00Z"
}
```

### 4. **API Routes**

**No dedicated API routes exist for changelogs.** All operations go directly through Supabase client:
- Queries use Supabase `.from('changelogs')` and `.from('changelog_views')`
- No `/api/changelog/*` endpoints
- Telegram notifications are sent via `/api/telegram/send` when changelogs are published

### 5. **Changelog Service Methods**

**Core Methods:**
- `getAllChangelogs()` - Get all changelogs (admin only)
- `getPublishedChangelogs()` - Get published changelogs visible to all users
- `getUnreadChangelogs()` - Get published changelogs not yet viewed by current user
- `getChangelogById(id)` - Get specific changelog
- `createChangelog(data)` - Create new changelog
- `updateChangelog(id, data)` - Update existing changelog
- `deleteChangelog(id)` - Delete changelog
- `publishChangelog(id)` - Publish a draft
- `unpublishChangelog(id)` - Unpublish a changelog
- `markAsViewed(changelogId)` - Mark single changelog as viewed
- `markMultipleAsViewed(ids)` - Mark multiple changelogs as viewed

### 6. **Feature Highlights**

- **Role-Based Access:** Super admin only for management (`/app/admin/changelog/page.tsx`)
- **Publication Control:** Drafts can be created and scheduled for publishing
- **User Tracking:** System tracks which users have viewed which changelogs
- **Unread Badge:** Users only see unread changelogs in the modal
- **Telegram Integration:** Automatically sends Telegram notifications when changelogs are published
- **Automatic Modal:** Shows unread changelogs automatically when users log in
- **Navigation:** Users can browse through multiple unread changelogs with prev/next buttons
- **Dismiss Options:** Users can mark changelogs as read or dismiss without marking

### 7. **Integration Points**

- **Wrapped in Layout:** The `ChangelogProvider` and `ChangelogModal` are integrated in `/app/layout.tsx`
- **Auth Context:** Uses `useAuth()` to get current user for tracking views
- **Toast Notifications:** Success/error messages via toast UI component

### 8. **Git History**

- **Key Commit:** `98e5b35` (v0.1.1) - Added full changelog system
- **Recent Commit:** `22f42a0` - Added Telegram notification integration
- **Introduction Date:** December 27, 2025

This is a complete, production-ready changelog system with admin management, user tracking, and notification integration!

 `./lib/changelogService.ts`

```text
     1→import { supabase } from './supabase';
     2→
     3→// TypeScript types for Changelog
     4→export interface Changelog {
     5→  id: string;
     6→  version: string;
     7→  title: string;
     8→  content: string;
     9→  published_at: string | null;
    10→  is_published: boolean;
    11→  created_by: string | null;
    12→  created_at: string;
    13→  updated_at: string;
    14→}
    15→
    16→export interface CreateChangelogData {
    17→  version: string;
    18→  title: string;
    19→  content: string;
    20→  published_at?: string | null;
    21→  is_published?: boolean;
    22→}
    23→
    24→export interface UpdateChangelogData {
    25→  version?: string;
    26→  title?: string;
    27→  content?: string;
    28→  published_at?: string | null;
    29→  is_published?: boolean;
    30→}
    31→
    32→export class ChangelogService {
    33→  /**
    34→   * Get all changelogs (admin only - includes drafts)
    35→   */
    36→  static async getAllChangelogs(): Promise<Changelog[]> {
    37→    const { data, error } = await supabase
    38→      .from('changelogs')
    39→      .select('*')
    40→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
    41→
    42→    if (error) {
    43→      console.error('Error fetching changelogs:', error);
    44→      throw error;
    45→    }
    46→
    47→    return data || [];
    48→  }
    49→
    50→  /**
    51→   * Get all published changelogs (visible to all users)
    52→   */
    53→  static async getPublishedChangelogs(): Promise<Changelog[]> {
    54→    const { data, error } = await supabase
    55→      .from('changelogs')
    56→      .select('*')
    57→      .eq('is_published', true)
    58→      .or('published_at.is.null,published_at.lte.now()')
    59→      .order('published_at', { ascending: false, nullsFirst: false });
    60→
    61→    if (error) {
    62→      console.error('Error fetching published changelogs:', error);
    63→      throw error;
    64→    }
    65→
    66→    return data || [];
    67→  }
    68→
    69→  /**
    70→   * Get unread changelogs for the current user
    71→   */
    72→  static async getUnreadChangelogs(): Promise<Changelog[]> {
    73→    // Get current user
    74→    const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser();
    75→    if (!user) return [];
    76→
    77→    // First, get all changelog IDs that the user has viewed
    78→    const { data: viewedData, error: viewedError } = await supabase
    79→      .from('changelog_views')
    80→      .select('changelog_id')
    81→      .eq('user_id', user.id);
    82→
    83→    if (viewedError) {
    84→      console.error('Error fetching viewed changelogs:', viewedError);
    85→      throw viewedError;
    86→    }
    87→
    88→    const viewedIds = (viewedData || []).map(v => v.changelog_id);
    89→
    90→    // Get all published changelogs
    91→    let query = supabase
    92→      .from('changelogs')
    93→      .select('*')
    94→      .eq('is_published', true)
    95→      .or('published_at.is.null,published_at.lte.now()')
    96→      .order('published_at', { ascending: false, nullsFirst: false });
    97→
    98→    // Exclude viewed changelogs if any
    99→    if (viewedIds.length > 0) {
   100→      query = query.not('id', 'in', `(${viewedIds.join(',')})`);
   101→    }
   102→
   103→    const { data, error } = await query;
   104→
   105→    if (error) {
   106→      console.error('Error fetching unread changelogs:', error);
   107→      throw error;
   108→    }
   109→
   110→    return data || [];
   111→  }
   112→
   113→  /**
   114→   * Get a single changelog by ID
   115→   */
   116→  static async getChangelogById(id: string): Promise<Changelog | null> {
   117→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   118→      .from('changelogs')
   119→      .select('*')
   120→      .eq('id', id)
   121→      .single();
   122→
   123→    if (error) {
   124→      if (error.code === 'PGRST116') return null; // Not found
   125→      console.error('Error fetching changelog:', error);
   126→      throw error;
   127→    }
   128→
   129→    return data;
   130→  }
   131→
   132→  /**
   133→   * Create a new changelog (admin only)
   134→   */
   135→  static async createChangelog(data: CreateChangelogData): Promise<Changelog> {
   136→    const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser();
   137→
   138→    const { data: changelog, error } = await supabase
   139→      .from('changelogs')
   140→      .insert({
   141→        ...data,
   142→        created_by: user?.id,
   143→        created_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   144→        updated_at: new Date().toISOString()
   145→      })
   146→      .select()
   147→      .single();
   148→
   149→    if (error) {
   150→      console.error('Error creating changelog:', error);
   151→      throw error;
   152→    }
   153→
   154→    return changelog;
   155→  }
   156→
   157→  /**
   158→   * Update an existing changelog (admin only)
   159→   */
   160→  static async updateChangelog(id: string, data: UpdateChangelogData): Promise<Changelog> {
   161→    const { data: changelog, error } = await supabase
   162→      .from('changelogs')
   163→      .update({
   164→        ...data,
   165→        updated_at: new Date().toISOString()
   166→      })
   167→      .eq('id', id)
   168→      .select()
   169→      .single();
   170→
   171→    if (error) {
   172→      console.error('Error updating changelog:', error);
   173→      throw error;
   174→    }
   175→
   176→    return changelog;
   177→  }
   178→
   179→  /**
   180→   * Delete a changelog (admin only)
   181→   */
   182→  static async deleteChangelog(id: string): Promise<void> {
   183→    const { error } = await supabase
   184→      .from('changelogs')
   185→      .delete()
   186→      .eq('id', id);
   187→
   188→    if (error) {
   189→      console.error('Error deleting changelog:', error);
   190→      throw error;
   191→    }
   192→  }
   193→
   194→  /**
   195→   * Publish a changelog (admin only)
   196→   */
   197→  static async publishChangelog(id: string): Promise<Changelog> {
   198→    return this.updateChangelog(id, {
   199→      is_published: true,
   200→      published_at: new Date().toISOString()
   201→    });
   202→  }
   203→
   204→  /**
   205→   * Unpublish a changelog (admin only)
   206→   */
   207→  static async unpublishChangelog(id: string): Promise<Changelog> {
   208→    return this.updateChangelog(id, {
   209→      is_published: false
   210→    });
   211→  }
   212→
   213→  /**
   214→   * Mark a changelog as viewed by the current user
   215→   */
   216→  static async markAsViewed(changelogId: string): Promise<void> {
   217→    const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser();
   218→    if (!user) return;
   219→
   220→    const { error } = await supabase
   221→      .from('changelog_views')
   222→      .upsert({
   223→        user_id: user.id,
   224→        changelog_id: changelogId,
   225→        viewed_at: new Date().toISOString()
   226→      }, {
   227→        onConflict: 'user_id,changelog_id'
   228→      });
   229→
   230→    if (error) {
   231→      console.error('Error marking changelog as viewed:', error);
   232→      throw error;
   233→    }
   234→  }
   235→
   236→  /**
   237→   * Mark multiple changelogs as viewed by the current user
   238→   */
   239→  static async markMultipleAsViewed(changelogIds: string[]): Promise<void> {
   240→    const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser();
   241→    if (!user || changelogIds.length === 0) return;
   242→
   243→    const records = changelogIds.map(changelogId => ({
   244→      user_id: user.id,
   245→      changelog_id: changelogId,
   246→      viewed_at: new Date().toISOString()
   247→    }));
   248→
   249→    const { error } = await supabase
   250→      .from('changelog_views')
   251→      .upsert(records, {
   252→        onConflict: 'user_id,changelog_id'
   253→      });
   254→
   255→    if (error) {
   256→      console.error('Error marking changelogs as viewed:', error);
   257→      throw error;
   258→    }
   259→  }
   260→}
   261→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→'use client';
     2→
     3→import React, { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
     4→import {
     5→  Dialog,
     6→  DialogContent,
     7→  DialogHeader,
     8→  DialogTitle,
     9→  DialogDescription,
    10→  DialogFooter,
    11→} from '@/components/ui/dialog';
    12→import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';
    13→import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge';
    14→import { ScrollArea } from '@/components/ui/scroll-area';
    15→import { Checkbox } from '@/components/ui/checkbox';
    16→import { Label } from '@/components/ui/label';
    17→import { Sparkles, ChevronLeft, ChevronRight } from 'lucide-react';
    18→import { Changelog } from '@/lib/changelogService';
    19→import { useAuth } from '@/contexts/AuthContext';
    20→import { useChangelog } from '@/contexts/ChangelogContext';
    21→
    22→export default function ChangelogModal() {
    23→  const { user } = useAuth();
    24→  const { unreadChangelogs, loading, markAsViewed } = useChangelog();
    25→  const [isOpen, setIsOpen] = useState(false);
    26→  const [displayedChangelogs, setDisplayedChangelogs] = useState<Changelog[]>([]);
    27→  const [currentIndex, setCurrentIndex] = useState(0);
    28→  const [markAsRead, setMarkAsRead] = useState(true);
    29→
    30→  // Show modal when there are unread changelogs
    31→  useEffect(() => {
    32→    if (user && !loading && unreadChangelogs.length > 0 && displayedChangelogs.length === 0) {
    33→      setDisplayedChangelogs(unreadChangelogs);
    34→      setCurrentIndex(0);
    35→      setIsOpen(true);
    36→    }
    37→  }, [user, loading, unreadChangelogs, displayedChangelogs.length]);
    38→
    39→  const handleDismiss = async () => {
    40→    // Only mark as viewed if checkbox is checked
    41→    if (markAsRead) {
    42→      const ids = displayedChangelogs.map(c => c.id);
    43→      await markAsViewed(ids);
    44→    }
    45→    setIsOpen(false);
    46→    setDisplayedChangelogs([]);
    47→    setMarkAsRead(true); // Reset for next time
    48→  };
    49→
    50→  const handlePrevious = () => {
    51→    setCurrentIndex(prev => Math.max(0, prev - 1));
    52→  };
    53→
    54→  const handleNext = () => {
    55→    setCurrentIndex(prev => Math.min(displayedChangelogs.length - 1, prev + 1));
    56→  };
    57→
    58→  if (!user || displayedChangelogs.length === 0) {
    59→    return null;
    60→  }
    61→
    62→  const currentChangelog = displayedChangelogs[currentIndex];
    63→  const hasMultiple = displayedChangelogs.length > 1;
    64→
    65→  // Format date
    66→  const formatDate = (dateString: string | null) => {
    67→    if (!dateString) return '';
    68→    return new Date(dateString).toLocaleDateString('en-US', {
    69→      year: 'numeric',
    70→      month: 'long',
    71→      day: 'numeric'
    72→    });
    73→  };
    74→
    75→  // Simple markdown-like rendering (bold, newlines, bullet points)
    76→  const renderContent = (content: string) => {
    77→    return content.split('\n').map((line, index) => {
    78→      // Handle bullet points
    79→      if (line.trim().startsWith('- ') || line.trim().startsWith('* ')) {
    80→        return (
    81→          <li key={index} className="ml-4 text-gray-700">
    82→            {line.trim().substring(2)}
    83→          </li>
    84→        );
    85→      }
    86→      // Handle headers (## or ###)
    87→      if (line.trim().startsWith('### ')) {
    88→        return (
    89→          <h4 key={index} className="font-semibold text-gray-900 mt-3 mb-1">
    90→            {line.trim().substring(4)}
    91→          </h4>
    92→        );
    93→      }
    94→      if (line.trim().startsWith('## ')) {
    95→        return (
    96→          <h3 key={index} className="font-bold text-gray-900 mt-4 mb-2">
    97→            {line.trim().substring(3)}
    98→          </h3>
    99→        );
   100→      }
   101→      // Empty lines become spacing
   102→      if (line.trim() === '') {
   103→        return <div key={index} className="h-2" />;
   104→      }
   105→      // Regular paragraphs
   106→      return (
   107→        <p key={index} className="text-gray-700">
   108→          {line}
   109→        </p>
   110→      );
   111→    });
   112→  };
   113→
   114→  return (
   115→    <Dialog open={isOpen} onOpenChange={(open) => !open && handleDismiss()}>
   116→      <DialogContent className="sm:max-w-lg">
   117→        <DialogHeader>
   118→          <div className="flex items-center gap-2 mb-1">
   119→            <Sparkles className="h-5 w-5 text-[#3e8692]" />
   120→            <DialogTitle className="text-xl">What's New</DialogTitle>
   121→          </div>
   122→          <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
   123→            <Badge
   124→              variant="secondary"
   125→              className="bg-[#3e8692]/10 text-[#3e8692] hover:bg-[#3e8692]/20"
   126→            >
   127→              v{currentChangelog.version}
   128→            </Badge>
   129→            {currentChangelog.published_at && (
   130→              <span className="text-sm text-gray-500">
   131→                {formatDate(currentChangelog.published_at)}
   132→              </span>
   133→            )}
   134→          </div>
   135→          <DialogDescription className="text-base font-medium text-gray-900 mt-2">
   136→            {currentChangelog.title}
   137→          </DialogDescription>
   138→        </DialogHeader>
   139→
   140→        <ScrollArea className="max-h-[300px] pr-4">
   141→          <div className="space-y-1">
   142→            {renderContent(currentChangelog.content)}
   143→          </div>
   144→        </ScrollArea>
   145→
   146→        <DialogFooter className="flex-col gap-4">
   147→          {/* Mark as read checkbox */}
   148→          <div className="flex items-center space-x-2 w-full">
   149→            <Checkbox
   150→              id="markAsRead"
   151→              checked={markAsRead}
   152→              onCheckedChange={(checked) => setMarkAsRead(checked === true)}
   153→            />
   154→            <Label
   155→              htmlFor="markAsRead"
   156→              className="text-sm text-gray-500 cursor-pointer"
   157→            >
   158→              Don't show this update again
   159→            </Label>
   160→          </div>
   161→
   162→          {/* Navigation and dismiss button */}
   163→          <div className="flex items-center justify-between w-full">
   164→            {hasMultiple ? (
   165→              <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
   166→                <Button
   167→                  variant="outline"
   168→                  size="sm"
   169→                  onClick={handlePrevious}
   170→                  disabled={currentIndex === 0}
   171→                >
   172→                  <ChevronLeft className="h-4 w-4" />
   173→                </Button>
   174→                <span className="text-sm text-gray-500">
   175→                  {currentIndex + 1} of {displayedChangelogs.length}
   176→                </span>
   177→                <Button
   178→                  variant="outline"
   179→                  size="sm"
   180→                  onClick={handleNext}
   181→                  disabled={currentIndex === displayedChangelogs.length - 1}
   182→                >
   183→                  <ChevronRight className="h-4 w-4" />
   184→                </Button>
   185→              </div>
   186→            ) : (
   187→              <div />
   188→            )}
   189→            <Button
   190→              onClick={handleDismiss}
   191→              style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692' }}
   192→              className="text-white hover:opacity-90"
   193→            >
   194→              Got it!
   195→            </Button>
   196→          </div>
   197→        </DialogFooter>
   198→      </DialogContent>
   199→    </Dialog>
   200→  );
   201→}
   202→
```
</details></tool-use>

     1→'use client';
     2→
     3→import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
     4→import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';
     5→import { Input } from '@/components/ui/input';
     6→import { Label } from '@/components/ui/label';
     7→import { Textarea } from '@/components/ui/textarea';
     8→import { Card, CardContent } from '@/components/ui/card';
     9→import { Dialog, DialogContent, DialogDescription, DialogFooter, DialogHeader, DialogTitle } from '@/components/ui/dialog';
    10→import { Alert, AlertDescription } from '@/components/ui/alert';
    11→import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge';
    12→import { Switch } from '@/components/ui/switch';
    13→import { Skeleton } from '@/components/ui/skeleton';
    14→import { ChangelogService, Changelog, CreateChangelogData } from '@/lib/changelogService';
    15→import { useAuth } from '@/contexts/AuthContext';
    16→import { useToast } from '@/hooks/use-toast';
    17→import { useRouter } from 'next/navigation';
    18→import { Plus, Edit, Trash2, Eye, EyeOff, Sparkles, ShieldAlert } from 'lucide-react';
    19→
    20→export default function ChangelogAdminPage() {
    21→  const { userProfile } = useAuth();
    22→  const router = useRouter();
    23→  const { toast } = useToast();
    24→
    25→  const [changelogs, setChangelogs] = useState<Changelog[]>([]);
    26→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
    27→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
    28→  const [isDialogOpen, setIsDialogOpen] = useState(false);
    29→  const [editingChangelog, setEditingChangelog] = useState<Changelog | null>(null);
    30→  const [formData, setFormData] = useState<CreateChangelogData>({
    31→    version: '',
    32→    title: '',
    33→    content: '',
    34→    is_published: false
    35→  });
    36→  const [saving, setSaving] = useState(false);
    37→
    38→  // Check if user is super_admin
    39→  const isSuperAdmin = userProfile?.role === 'super_admin';
    40→
    41→  useEffect(() => {
    42→    if (userProfile && !isSuperAdmin) {
    43→      // Redirect non-super_admins
    44→      router.push('/');
    45→      return;
    46→    }
    47→    if (userProfile && isSuperAdmin) {
    48→      fetchChangelogs();
    49→    }
    50→  }, [userProfile, isSuperAdmin, router]);
    51→
    52→  const fetchChangelogs = async () => {
    53→    try {
    54→      setLoading(true);
    55→      const data = await ChangelogService.getAllChangelogs();
    56→      setChangelogs(data);
    57→    } catch (err) {
    58→      setError('Failed to fetch changelogs');
    59→      console.error('Error fetching changelogs:', err);
    60→    } finally {
    61→      setLoading(false);
    62→    }
    63→  };
    64→
    65→  const openCreateDialog = () => {
    66→    setEditingChangelog(null);
    67→    setFormData({
    68→      version: '',
    69→      title: '',
    70→      content: '',
    71→      is_published: false
    72→    });
    73→    setIsDialogOpen(true);
    74→  };
    75→
    76→  const openEditDialog = (changelog: Changelog) => {
    77→    setEditingChangelog(changelog);
    78→    setFormData({
    79→      version: changelog.version,
    80→      title: changelog.title,
    81→      content: changelog.content,
    82→      is_published: changelog.is_published
    83→    });
    84→    setIsDialogOpen(true);
    85→  };
    86→
    87→  const handleSave = async () => {
    88→    try {
    89→      if (!formData.version.trim() || !formData.title.trim() || !formData.content.trim()) {
    90→        toast({
    91→          title: 'Error',
    92→          description: 'Version, title, and content are required',
    93→          variant: 'destructive',
    94→        });
    95→        return;
    96→      }
    97→
    98→      setSaving(true);
    99→
   100→      if (editingChangelog) {
   101→        const wasPublished = editingChangelog.is_published;
   102→        await ChangelogService.updateChangelog(editingChangelog.id, {
   103→          version: formData.version,
   104→          title: formData.title,
   105→          content: formData.content,
   106→          is_published: formData.is_published,
   107→          published_at: formData.is_published ? new Date().toISOString() : null
   108→        });
   109→
   110→        // Send Telegram notification if switching from draft to published
   111→        if (!wasPublished && formData.is_published) {
   112→          try {
   113→            const notificationMessage = `<b>New Changelog Published</b>\n\n<b>Version:</b> v${formData.version}\n<b>Title:</b> ${formData.title}\n\n${formData.content.substring(0, 500)}${formData.content.length > 500 ? '...' : ''}`;
   114→
   115→            await fetch('/api/telegram/send', {
   116→              method: 'POST',
   117→              headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   118→              body: JSON.stringify({ message: notificationMessage })
   119→            });
   120→          } catch (telegramErr) {
   121→            console.error('Failed to send Telegram notification:', telegramErr);
   122→          }
   123→        }
   124→
   125→        toast({
   126→          title: 'Success',
   127→          description: 'Changelog updated successfully',
   128→        });
   129→      } else {
   130→        await ChangelogService.createChangelog({
   131→          version: formData.version,
   132→          title: formData.title,
   133→          content: formData.content,
   134→          is_published: formData.is_published,
   135→          published_at: formData.is_published ? new Date().toISOString() : null
   136→        });
   137→
   138→        // Send Telegram notification if created as published
   139→        if (formData.is_published) {
   140→          try {
   141→            const notificationMessage = `<b>New Changelog Published</b>\n\n<b>Version:</b> v${formData.version}\n<b>Title:</b> ${formData.title}\n\n${formData.content.substring(0, 500)}${formData.content.length > 500 ? '...' : ''}`;
   142→
   143→            await fetch('/api/telegram/send', {
   144→              method: 'POST',
   145→              headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   146→              body: JSON.stringify({ message: notificationMessage })
   147→            });
   148→          } catch (telegramErr) {
   149→            console.error('Failed to send Telegram notification:', telegramErr);
   150→          }
   151→        }
   152→
   153→        toast({
   154→          title: 'Success',
   155→          description: 'Changelog created successfully',
   156→        });
   157→      }
   158→
   159→      await fetchChangelogs();
   160→      setIsDialogOpen(false);
   161→    } catch (err) {
   162→      toast({
   163→        title: 'Error',
   164→        description: 'Failed to save changelog',
   165→        variant: 'destructive',
   166→      });
   167→    } finally {
   168→      setSaving(false);
   169→    }
   170→  };
   171→
   172→  const handleDelete = async (id: string) => {
   173→    if (!confirm('Are you sure you want to delete this changelog?')) return;
   174→
   175→    try {
   176→      await ChangelogService.deleteChangelog(id);
   177→      await fetchChangelogs();
   178→      toast({
   179→        title: 'Success',
   180→        description: 'Changelog deleted successfully',
   181→      });
   182→    } catch (err) {
   183→      toast({
   184→        title: 'Error',
   185→        description: 'Failed to delete changelog',
   186→        variant: 'destructive',
   187→      });
   188→    }
   189→  };
   190→
   191→  const handleTogglePublish = async (changelog: Changelog) => {
   192→    try {
   193→      if (changelog.is_published) {
   194→        await ChangelogService.unpublishChangelog(changelog.id);
   195→        toast({
   196→          title: 'Success',
   197→          description: 'Changelog unpublished',
   198→        });
   199→      } else {
   200→        await ChangelogService.publishChangelog(changelog.id);
   201→
   202→        // Send Telegram notification to HH Operations chat
   203→        try {
   204→          const notificationMessage = `<b>New Changelog Published</b>\n\n<b>Version:</b> v${changelog.version}\n<b>Title:</b> ${changelog.title}\n\n${changelog.content.substring(0, 500)}${changelog.content.length > 500 ? '...' : ''}`;
   205→
   206→          await fetch('/api/telegram/send', {
   207→            method: 'POST',
   208→            headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   209→            body: JSON.stringify({ message: notificationMessage })
   210→          });
   211→        } catch (telegramErr) {
   212→          console.error('Failed to send Telegram notification:', telegramErr);
   213→          // Don't fail the publish if notification fails
   214→        }
   215→
   216→        toast({
   217→          title: 'Success',
   218→          description: 'Changelog published',
   219→        });
   220→      }
   221→      await fetchChangelogs();
   222→    } catch (err) {
   223→      toast({
   224→        title: 'Error',
   225→        description: 'Failed to update changelog status',
   226→        variant: 'destructive',
   227→      });
   228→    }
   229→  };
   230→
   231→  const formatDate = (dateString: string | null) => {
   232→    if (!dateString) return 'Not published';
   233→    return new Date(dateString).toLocaleDateString('en-US', {
   234→      year: 'numeric',
   235→      month: 'short',
   236→      day: 'numeric',
   237→      hour: '2-digit',
   238→      minute: '2-digit'
   239→    });
   240→  };
   241→
   242→  // Show access denied for non-super_admins
   243→  if (userProfile && !isSuperAdmin) {
   244→    return (
   245→      <div className="flex flex-col h-full gap-6">
   246→        <Card>
   247→          <CardContent className="py-12 text-center">
   248→            <ShieldAlert className="h-12 w-12 text-red-400 mx-auto mb-4" />
   249→            <h3 className="text-lg font-medium text-gray-900 mb-2">Access Denied</h3>
   250→            <p className="text-gray-500 max-w-md mx-auto">
   251→              Only super admins can access the changelog management page.
   252→            </p>
   253→          </CardContent>
   254→        </Card>
   255→      </div>
   256→    );
   257→  }
   258→
   259→  if (loading || !userProfile) {
   260→    return (
   261→      <div className="flex flex-col h-full gap-6">
   262→        <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   263→          <div>
   264→            <Skeleton className="h-8 w-48 mb-2" />
   265→            <Skeleton className="h-5 w-72" />
   266→          </div>
   267→          <Skeleton className="h-10 w-32" />
   268→        </div>
   269→        <div className="space-y-4">
   270→          <Skeleton className="h-32 w-full" />
   271→          <Skeleton className="h-32 w-full" />
   272→          <Skeleton className="h-32 w-full" />
   273→        </div>
   274→      </div>
   275→    );
   276→  }
   277→
   278→  return (
   279→    <div className="flex flex-col h-full gap-6">
   280→      <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   281→        <div>
   282→          <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900">Changelog Management</h2>
   283→          <p className="text-gray-600">Create and manage portal update announcements</p>
   284→        </div>
   285→        <Button
   286→          onClick={openCreateDialog}
   287→          style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }}
   288→          className="hover:opacity-90"
   289→        >
   290→          <Plus className="h-4 w-4 mr-2" />
   291→          New Changelog
   292→        </Button>
   293→      </div>
   294→
   295→      {error && (
   296→        <Alert variant="destructive">
   297→          <AlertDescription>{error}</AlertDescription>
   298→        </Alert>
   299→      )}
   300→
   301→      {changelogs.length === 0 ? (
   302→        <Card>
   303→          <CardContent className="py-12 text-center">
   304→            <Sparkles className="h-12 w-12 text-gray-300 mx-auto mb-4" />
   305→            <h3 className="text-lg font-medium text-gray-900 mb-2">No changelogs yet</h3>
   306→            <p className="text-gray-500 max-w-md mx-auto mb-4">
   307→              Create your first changelog to announce updates to your users.
   308→            </p>
   309→            <Button
   310→              onClick={openCreateDialog}
   311→              style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }}
   312→              className="hover:opacity-90"
   313→            >
   314→              <Plus className="h-4 w-4 mr-2" />
   315→              Create First Changelog
   316→            </Button>
   317→          </CardContent>
   318→        </Card>
   319→      ) : (
   320→        <div className="grid gap-4">
   321→          {changelogs.map((changelog) => (
   322→            <Card key={changelog.id} className="hover:shadow-md transition-shadow">
   323→              <CardContent className="p-4">
   324→                <div className="flex items-start justify-between gap-4">
   325→                  <div className="flex-1 min-w-0">
   326→                    <div className="flex items-center gap-3 mb-2">
   327→                      <Badge
   328→                        variant="secondary"
   329→                        className="bg-[#3e8692]/10 text-[#3e8692]"
   330→                      >
   331→                        v{changelog.version}
   332→                      </Badge>
   333→                      <Badge
   334→                        variant={changelog.is_published ? 'default' : 'secondary'}
   335→                        style={changelog.is_published ? { backgroundColor: '#3e8692' } : {}}
   336→                      >
   337→                        {changelog.is_published ? 'Published' : 'Draft'}
   338→                      </Badge>
   339→                    </div>
   340→                    <h3 className="font-semibold text-gray-900 mb-1">
   341→                      {changelog.title}
   342→                    </h3>
   343→                    <p className="text-sm text-gray-500 line-clamp-2 mb-2">
   344→                      {changelog.content}
   345→                    </p>
   346→                    <div className="text-xs text-gray-400">
   347→                      {changelog.is_published
   348→                        ? `Published ${formatDate(changelog.published_at)}`
   349→                        : `Created ${formatDate(changelog.created_at)}`
   350→                      }
   351→                    </div>
   352→                  </div>
   353→
   354→                  <div className="flex items-center gap-2 flex-shrink-0">
   355→                    <Button
   356→                      variant="outline"
   357→                      size="sm"
   358→                      onClick={() => handleTogglePublish(changelog)}
   359→                      title={changelog.is_published ? 'Unpublish' : 'Publish'}
   360→                    >
   361→                      {changelog.is_published ? (
   362→                        <EyeOff className="h-4 w-4" />
   363→                      ) : (
   364→                        <Eye className="h-4 w-4" />
   365→                      )}
   366→                    </Button>
   367→                    <Button
   368→                      variant="outline"
   369→                      size="sm"
   370→                      onClick={() => openEditDialog(changelog)}
   371→                    >
   372→                      <Edit className="h-4 w-4" />
   373→                    </Button>
   374→                    <Button
   375→                      variant="outline"
   376→                      size="sm"
   377→                      onClick={() => handleDelete(changelog.id)}
   378→                      className="hover:bg-red-50 hover:text-red-700"
   379→                    >
   380→                      <Trash2 className="h-4 w-4" />
   381→                    </Button>
   382→                  </div>
   383→                </div>
   384→              </CardContent>
   385→            </Card>
   386→          ))}
   387→        </div>
   388→      )}
   389→
   390→      {/* Create/Edit Dialog */}
   391→      <Dialog open={isDialogOpen} onOpenChange={setIsDialogOpen}>
   392→        <DialogContent className="sm:max-w-lg">
   393→          <DialogHeader>
   394→            <DialogTitle>
   395→              {editingChangelog ? 'Edit Changelog' : 'Create New Changelog'}
   396→            </DialogTitle>
   397→            <DialogDescription>
   398→              {editingChangelog
   399→                ? 'Update the changelog entry details.'
   400→                : 'Create a new changelog entry to announce updates to users.'
   401→              }
   402→            </DialogDescription>
   403→          </DialogHeader>
   404→
   405→          <div className="space-y-4 py-4">
   406→            <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-4">
   407→              <div className="space-y-2">
   408→                <Label htmlFor="version">Version</Label>
   409→                <Input
   410→                  id="version"
   411→                  value={formData.version}
   412→                  onChange={(e) => setFormData(prev => ({ ...prev, version: e.target.value }))}
   413→                  placeholder="e.g., 1.2.0"
   414→                  className="auth-input"
   415→                />
   416→              </div>
   417→              <div className="space-y-2 flex items-end">
   418→                <div className="flex items-center space-x-2">
   419→                  <Switch
   420→                    id="is_published"
   421→                    checked={formData.is_published}
   422→                    onCheckedChange={(checked) => setFormData(prev => ({ ...prev, is_published: checked }))}
   423→                  />
   424→                  <Label htmlFor="is_published">
   425→                    {formData.is_published ? 'Published' : 'Draft'}
   426→                  </Label>
   427→                </div>
   428→              </div>
   429→            </div>
   430→
   431→            <div className="space-y-2">
   432→              <Label htmlFor="title">Title</Label>
   433→              <Input
   434→                id="title"
   435→                value={formData.title}
   436→                onChange={(e) => setFormData(prev => ({ ...prev, title: e.target.value }))}
   437→                placeholder="e.g., New Features & Improvements"
   438→                className="auth-input"
   439→              />
   440→            </div>
   441→
   442→            <div className="space-y-2">
   443→              <Label htmlFor="content">Content</Label>
   444→              <Textarea
   445→                id="content"
   446→                value={formData.content}
   447→                onChange={(e) => setFormData(prev => ({ ...prev, content: e.target.value }))}
   448→                placeholder="Describe the changes...
   449→
   450→Use markdown-like formatting:
   451→- Bullet points with - or *
   452→## Headers with ##
   453→### Subheaders with ###"
   454→                rows={8}
   455→                className="auth-input"
   456→              />
   457→              <p className="text-xs text-gray-500">
   458→                Supports simple formatting: bullet points (- or *), headers (## or ###)
   459→              </p>
   460→            </div>
   461→          </div>
   462→
   463→          <DialogFooter>
   464→            <Button variant="outline" onClick={() => setIsDialogOpen(false)}>
   465→              Cancel
   466→            </Button>
   467→            <Button
   468→              onClick={handleSave}
   469→              disabled={saving}
   470→              style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }}
   471→              className="hover:opacity-90"
   472→            >
   473→              {saving ? 'Saving...' : (editingChangelog ? 'Update' : 'Create')}
   474→            </Button>
   475→          </DialogFooter>
   476→        </DialogContent>
   477→      </Dialog>
   478→    </div>
   479→  );
   480→}
   481→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-03-12 17:03:13Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./contexts/ChangelogContext.tsx`

```text
     1→'use client'
     2→
     3→import { createContext, useContext, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     4→import { ChangelogService, Changelog } from '@/lib/changelogService'
     5→import { useAuth } from './AuthContext'
     6→
     7→interface ChangelogContextType {
     8→  changelogs: Changelog[]
     9→  unreadChangelogs: Changelog[]
    10→  latestVersion: string | null
    11→  loading: boolean
    12→  markAsViewed: (ids: string[]) => Promise<void>
    13→  refreshChangelogs: () => Promise<void>
    14→}
    15→
    16→const ChangelogContext = createContext<ChangelogContextType>({
    17→  changelogs: [],
    18→  unreadChangelogs: [],
    19→  latestVersion: null,
    20→  loading: true,
    21→  markAsViewed: async () => {},
    22→  refreshChangelogs: async () => {},
    23→})
    24→
    25→export const useChangelog = () => {
    26→  const context = useContext(ChangelogContext)
    27→  if (!context) {
    28→    throw new Error('useChangelog must be used within a ChangelogProvider')
    29→  }
    30→  return context
    31→}
    32→
    33→export const ChangelogProvider = ({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) => {
    34→  const { user } = useAuth()
    35→  const [changelogs, setChangelogs] = useState<Changelog[]>([])
    36→  const [unreadChangelogs, setUnreadChangelogs] = useState<Changelog[]>([])
    37→  const [latestVersion, setLatestVersion] = useState<string | null>(null)
    38→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true)
    39→  const [hasFetched, setHasFetched] = useState(false)
    40→
    41→  const fetchChangelogs = async () => {
    42→    try {
    43→      setLoading(true)
    44→      const published = await ChangelogService.getPublishedChangelogs()
    45→      setChangelogs(published)
    46→      if (published.length > 0) {
    47→        setLatestVersion(published[0].version)
    48→      }
    49→
    50→      // Fetch unread changelogs if user is logged in
    51→      if (user) {
    52→        const unread = await ChangelogService.getUnreadChangelogs()
    53→        setUnreadChangelogs(unread)
    54→      }
    55→    } catch (error) {
    56→      console.error('Error fetching changelogs:', error)
    57→    } finally {
    58→      setLoading(false)
    59→      setHasFetched(true)
    60→    }
    61→  }
    62→
    63→  // Fetch changelogs once when user is available
    64→  useEffect(() => {
    65→    if (user && !hasFetched) {
    66→      fetchChangelogs()
    67→    } else if (!user) {
    68→      // Reset when user logs out
    69→      setHasFetched(false)
    70→      setUnreadChangelogs([])
    71→    }
    72→  }, [user, hasFetched])
    73→
    74→  const markAsViewed = async (ids: string[]) => {
    75→    try {
    76→      await ChangelogService.markMultipleAsViewed(ids)
    77→      // Remove the viewed changelogs from unread list
    78→      setUnreadChangelogs(prev => prev.filter(c => !ids.includes(c.id)))
    79→    } catch (error) {
    80→      console.error('Error marking changelogs as viewed:', error)
    81→    }
    82→  }
    83→
    84→  const refreshChangelogs = async () => {
    85→    setHasFetched(false)
    86→    await fetchChangelogs()
    87→  }
    88→
    89→  const value = {
    90→    changelogs,
    91→    unreadChangelogs,
    92→    latestVersion,
    93→    loading,
    94→    markAsViewed,
    95→    refreshChangelogs,
    96→  }
    97→
    98→  return <ChangelogContext.Provider value={value}>{children}</ChangelogContext.Provider>
    99→}
   100→
```
</details></tool-use>

     1→import './globals.css';
     2→import type { Metadata } from 'next';
     3→import { Inter } from 'next/font/google';
     4→import { AuthProvider } from '@/contexts/AuthContext';
     5→import { ChangelogProvider } from '@/contexts/ChangelogContext';
     6→import { Toaster } from '@/components/ui/toaster';
     7→import ChangelogModal from '@/components/changelog/ChangelogModal';
     8→import { ChunkErrorHandler } from '@/components/ChunkErrorHandler';
     9→
    10→const inter = Inter({ subsets: ['latin'] });
    11→
    12→export const metadata: Metadata = {
    13→  title: 'Holo Hive Portal',
    14→  description: 'Manage your marketing campaigns and client relationships with Holo Hive',
    15→  icons: {
    16→    icon: '/images/logo.png',
    17→    shortcut: '/images/logo.png',
    18→    apple: '/images/logo.png',
    19→  },
    20→};
    21→
    22→export default function RootLayout({
    23→  children,
    24→}: {
    25→  children: React.ReactNode;
    26→}) {

    v0.1.1: CRM Enhancements, Changelog System & Bug Fixes
    
    ### CRM Pipeline - Accounts
    - Add client linking for accounts with new Client column
    - Add Scope field (Fundraising, Advisory, KOL Activation, GTM, BD/Partnerships, APAC)

    - Add Focus Area dropdown for Partners
    - Change commission model to dropdown (Standard, Strategic, Whitelabeled)

    ### Clients Page

    ### Changelog System
    - Add version badge in sidebar that opens changelog dialog
    - Add ChangelogProvider context to prevent redundant fetches
    
    ### Branding

    ### Bug Fixes
